title: HbA1c to Average Glucose Calculator 2026 | Hacé Cuentas
description: Convert HbA1c to estimated average glucose (eAG). Track blood sugar levels and diabetes risk with our free calculator.
intro: Reference values in healthcare come from large population studies (NHANES, WHO). They provide a framework to interpret your results, but your personal context matters most. Convert your HbA1c to estimated average glucose (eAG). Values are current as of 2026 and reviewed regularly to keep you updated.
keyTakeaway: Formula: **eAG = 28.7 × HbA1c - 46.7**. Enter your HbA1c percentage to get your estimated average glucose (eAG) in mg/dL.
useCases: Technical calculations for HbA1c to average glucose conversion
Teaching and learning glucose management concepts
explanation:
**Normal health reference values** come from large population studies (NHANES, WHO, CDC). They vary by age, sex, ethnicity, and region. They're guidelines only—your personal medical history always takes precedence.

## How It Works

This calculator converts HbA1c (hemoglobin A1c) to estimated average glucose (eAG).

The calculator applies the formula with your input values, checking typical ranges and alerting you if values fall outside normal limits.

## Normal Reference Ranges

| Parameter | Normal Adult Range |
|---|---|
| Blood Pressure | <120/80 mmHg |
| Resting Heart Rate | 60–100 bpm |
| Temperature | 36.1–37.2°C |
| SpO2 | 95–100% |
| Fasting Glucose | 70–100 mg/dL |
| HbA1c | <5.7% |
| Total Cholesterol | <200 mg/dL |
| LDL | <100 mg/dL |
| HDL | >40 (M) / >50 (F) |

## Final Notes

This calculation is for reference only. For critical decisions (financial, medical, legal), consult a specialized healthcare provider. Values are current as of 2026 and reviewed regularly.

faq.a: HbA1c is the percentage of hemoglobin bound to glucose. It reflects your average blood sugar levels over the past 3 months.

faq.q: What is a normal HbA1c level?

faq.a: eAG is your estimated average glucose level in mg/dL. Formula: 28.7 × HbA1c - 46.7.

faq.q: How often should I check my HbA1c?
faq.a: People with diabetes: every 3 months. Regular health monitoring: once a year.
Normal ranges stay consistent with age and don't change due to aging alone.

faq.q: What are HbA1c targets during pregnancy?
faq.a: Targets are stricter during pregnancy: ideally <6%.

faq.q: What HbA1c should I aim for?
faq.a: This depends on individual goals. Many doctors target <7% for people with diabetes, but discuss personalized targets with your provider.

faq.q: When should I see a doctor about my HbA1c?
faq.a: Contact your healthcare provider if values are outside normal ranges, you have associated symptoms, or have a family history of cardiovascular or metabolic disease.

sources.name: NIST — National Institute of Standards and Technology

dataUpdate.notes: Fixed formula, does not require external data updates
